lines: You crouch over him. A traveller on his back, knifed far past what killing takes, in a pool that has not finished drying. A silver cross still hangs at his throat. They did not want it.
His coat has been opened at the chest and the skin beneath it carved. A star, five lines, driven in point-down. Below it, cut slower and deeper, two lines: NOT HOLY. DO NOT FEAST.

You know the star. The Choir of the Open Wound. They open and eat only the ones they think are turning holy, the Host-touched they raise on their altars. This man was just faithful, a closed body with a cross. To the Choir that is refuse. They carved the words so the next of them would not waste a bite on him.
You draw his coat shut over the star. It does nothing for him. You do it anyway.
